top_headline: THE WEEK EVERYTHING LANDS — NVDA T-2 / FOMC 8-4 / BRENT $111 / HORMUZ CLOSED
chapters: I · MACRO (pages 02-03) — Warsh's first full week: TNX 4.63%, 30Y 5.1%+ (highest since May 2025); FOMC 8–4 hold Apr 29 (most divided since 1992); <3% June cut probability; 30–35% Dec hike probability (CME); SPX May 15 close 7,408.50 (−1.24%); NDX 26,225.14 (−1.54%); Asian risk-off (Hang Seng −1.49%, Nikkei ~−1.45%); FOMC minutes Wednesday 2pm ET

II · AI TRADE (pages 04-05) — NVDA T-2 to May 20: PM $229.72 (range $228–$233); Goldman EPS $1.86 vs $1.77 consensus; Citi models $1.4B Q1 upside to $80.2B vs $78.5B consensus; Q2 FY27 guide $86.08B = THE swing number; H200 China paradox: 10 firms licensed, 0 units shipped; Beijing directing domestic AI sourcing; Huawei+DeepSeek hold 41% China AI accelerator market; active flag no-009 ($219.44 May 13) at +4.7% unrealized PM

III · EARNINGS (pages 06-07) — Weekly docket May 18–22: BIDU Q1 today 8am (EPS $1.69, Rev $4.66B, Apollo Go robotaxi key); HD Q1 tomorrow BMO ($41.5B, $3.41 EPS, −4% YoY, mgmt guided down); NVDA Wed AH ($78.5B, $1.77 EPS, Q2 guide $86B key); LOW Wed BMO (housing headwinds); TJX Wed BMO (consumer trade-down beneficiary); INTU Wed AH (AI ARPU); WMT Thu BMO (grocery share gains); FOMC Minutes Wed 2pm (8–4 dissent autopsy)

IV · GEOPOLITICS (page 08) — Hormuz effectively closed: Brent $110.74/bbl Mon (range $110.13–$111.99); May 15 close $109.26 (+3.35%, +8.1% wk); 4M bbl/day flow loss; IEA undersupplied through October; Iran ceasefire structurally dead; Trump: 'get moving, FAST'; Gold $4,495/oz (falling despite geopolitical tension — dollar + yields dominating); DXY 99.35

tase_events.event: TA-35 / TA-125 watch: Brent $111/bbl sustained = direct Israeli energy import cost pressure; Iranian escalation and Hormuz closure = heightened regional security premium for Israeli defense sector (Elbit/Rafael/IWI); TNX 4.63% + risk-off global tone = headwind for TA-35 growth names

narrative_threads: Warsh's wall: Fed Chair enters first full week with TNX 4.63%, 30Y 5.1%+, Brent $111, and VIX 18.4 rising — the market is not complying with the rate-cut mandate he was appointed to pursue; FOMC 8–4 minutes Wednesday may formalize a hawkish pivot in language

NVDA May 20 binary: H200 China paradox is the defining overhang — US approved 10 firms, Beijing blocked all shipments; Wednesday earnings call is the ONLY forum where management provides China guidance; Q2 FY27 guide of $86B+ is the true bar the market watches

Hormuz structural closure: Ceasefire dead after weeks on 'massive life support'; Brent $111/bbl compounds Warsh's already-impossible inflation inheritance (CPI 3.8% + PPI 6.0%); IEA undersupply through October means no near-term relief without a diplomatic breakthrough

Earnings week finale: Five sessions, eight major reports, FOMC minutes, and NVDA — the densest catalyst week of the current earnings cycle; the week's collective output will set the risk tone through Memorial Day
